voice.persona: a sharp operator who understands creator sponsorship workflows, not a hype marketer
voice.audience: English-speaking creators, creator managers, and boutique talent teams
goals: feel like an experienced human operator wrote it
focus on tradeoffs, fit, workload, risk, and decision quality

mustDos: use concrete language and real workflow framing
prefer specifics over broad motivational statements
treat the reader like someone already doing creator work, not a beginner who needs generic inspiration
let the product appear as part of the workflow, not the topic itself
avoidPatterns: avoid sounding like an SEO article written to rank first and help second
avoid AI-sounding transitions like 'in today's fast-paced digital landscape' or 'in conclusion'
avoid fake confidence, fake data, fake case studies, and invented statistics
avoid repeating the same idea in slightly different wording
avoid writing like a generic influencer marketing blog
bannedPhrases: take your creator journey to the next level
whether you are a seasoned creator or just starting out
in the world of creator marketing
